name: Trail of the Riópar Factories
name_original: Riópar y Senda del Caz — recorrido circular
description: Hike the moderate 4.7-mile Riópar y Senda del Caz circular route through diverse landscapes and past historic Riópar Viejo.
region_intro: Hiking around Riópar offers access to diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Segura, characterized by its mountainous terrain, extensive pine forests, and significant natural features. The region is home to the Calares del Mundo and Sima Natural Park, where the Mundo River emerges from limestone formations, creating notable waterfalls. Hikers can explore valleys, hills, and peaks, with elevations ranging from lower riverbeds to summits like Almenara Peak. Best hiking trails around Riópar The most popular hiking route is Nacimiento del río Mundo — circular en el Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the source of the Mundo River, known for its limestone formations and waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the peaks of Almenara and Almenarilla from Riópar Viejo, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.1 km) path. This challenging route offers extensive views from the high peaks of the Sierra de Alcaraz. Local hikers also love the Riópar to El Pino del Toril — circular, a 12.5 miles (20.0 km) trail leading through pine forests and offering significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Riópar is defined by Sierra del Segura mountains, the Mundo River, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for easy family walks, moderate trails, and more challenging ascents to prominent peaks. The routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Riópar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Riópar?
A: Riópar offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 35 trails. These routes cater to all levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, allowing you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra del Segura.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Riópar?
A: The optimal times for hiking in Riópar are spring and autumn, when the weather is most pleasant. Spring, particularly around March, is also the ideal time to witness the spectacular 'reventón' – a sudden, massive burst of water – at the Source of the Mundo River.

Q: Are there any hikes in Riópar that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its waterfalls, especially the iconic Source of the Mundo River (Nacimiento del Río Mundo). Here, the river emerges from limestone rocks, forming breathtaking cascades like the 'Cascada de los Batanes' and 'Los Chorros waterfall'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063402208/nacimiento-del-rio-mundo-circular-en-el-parque-natural-de-los-calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Mundo — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent way to experience this natural wonder.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Riópar?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Riópar are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063402208/nacimiento-del-rio-mundo-circular-en-el-parque-natural-de-los-calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Mundo — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the river's source, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067584227/riopar-to-el-pino-del-toril-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar to El Pino del Toril — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through pine forests.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Riópar?
A: Yes, Riópar offers several accessible routes suitable for families. These trails often feature diverse plant life and provide gentle terrain, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty. The Riópar – Campamento de San Juan (RSR 1.1) is noted as an accessible route for families.

Q: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on hikes around Riópar?
A: The region is rich in stunning viewpoints and historical landmarks. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, an old village offering panoramic vistas of the Río de la Vega valley and the vast Calar del Mundo. For higher perspectives, consider trails leading to Almenara Peak (1,796 meters) or El Padroncillo (1,582 meters), which offer extensive views across the landscape and surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: Where can I find more challenging hikes in the Riópar area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Riópar provides several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368907960/ascent-to-the-peaks-of-almenara-and-almenarilla-from-riopar-viejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the peaks of Almenara and Almenarilla from Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.1 km path with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views from high peaks. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067584227/riopar-to-el-pino-del-toril-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar to El Pino del Toril — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 20 km trail with substantial elevation gain through pine forests.

Q: Are hiking trails in Riópar suitable for dogs?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check specific regulations for the Calares del Mundo and Sima Natural Park, as rules can vary. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept on a leash, but always ensure you clean up after your pet and respect local wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Riópar?
A: The hiking routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic waterfalls at the Source of the Mundo River to the serene pine forests and panoramic mountain views from peaks like Almenara.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, you can explore historical sites. The charming old village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a hill, offers not only stunning views but also a glimpse into the region's past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368900176/trail-of-the-riopar-factories" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Riópar Factories&lt;/a&gt; provides insight into the area's industrial heritage.

Q: What is the terrain like on Riópar's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Riópar is highly varied, reflecting its location within the Sierra del Segura. You'll encounter everything from gentle paths along riverbeds and through lush meadows to more rugged, rocky ascents in the higher mountain areas. Extensive pine forests are a common feature, and trails often involve moderate to significant elevation changes.
tips: Riópar is located in the Sierra de Alcaraz and has an impressive natural environment. In the town, it is worth seeing the Royal Factories of San Juan de Alcaraz, the Paseo de los Plátanos, the Plaza de Luiz Escudero, the Molino reservoir and the old town.
This aqueduct transported water from the Gollizo pond to the factories in Riópar. At present, only the infrastructure remains, which can be seen from the road.

The path follows the ditches that formerly transported water from the Toma del Agua to the factories in Riópar.

At present, it is common to find families walking through this place. It has no technical difficulty and offers you very nice views of the surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting and ending near Riópar. You can typically find parking within the town of Riópar itself, which serves as a convenient base for accessing trails in the area.

faqs.question: Is the Riópar y Senda del Caz — circular route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it's not overly long (about 7.6 km) and has a manageable elevation gain (around 116 meters), hikers should have good fitness. Paths are mostly accessible, but sure-footedness is required. It could be suitable for active families with children who are used to hiking.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse terrain characteristic of the Sierra de Alcaraz. Expect a mix of forest paths, meadows, and trails alongside streams. While mostly accessible, some sections may require sure-footedness. The path offers a rich natural environment with varied landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several interesting sights. You'll pass through the charming Plaza de Luis Escudero in Riópar. A significant part of the hike follows the Caz Trail, which includes the Caz de las Fábricas, a historical water channel. The area also boasts impressive natural scenery within the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the broader "Red de Senderos de Riópar" (Riópar Trail Network). Along the way, you'll intersect with sections of other official trails such as the Senda del Caz, Camino Histórico Paterna-Riópar, Calar del Rio Mundo, and the GR-66 Calar de la Osera/PR-AB 23 Paterna del Madera-Riópar.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. Given the route's location within a natural park, it's always best to keep your dog under control to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra del Segura region, where this trail is located, offers beautiful hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, though this specific route is at a lower altitude.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Riópar y Senda del Caz — circular route. It is located within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, which is freely accessible to the public for hiking. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience an impressive natural environment with extensive forests, meadows, streams, and springs. The region is known for its high biodiversity, boasting over 1,300 plant species and 174 cataloged vertebrate species. Keep an eye out for various birds, small mammals, and the rich flora of the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out for its accessibility to the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra del Segura, offering a manageable hike for those with moderate fitness. It also provides a unique blend of natural exploration with historical and cultural insights, passing near Riópar Viejo and the historical Caz de las Fábricas, reflecting the region's past in zinc and brass production.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or services available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends near Riópar, you'll find various amenities in the town. Riópar offers options for accommodation, restaurants, and cafes, making it a convenient place to refresh before or after your hike.
